entries.answerLong: Green jobs related to art include: Environmental Artist; Eco-Designer; Sustainable Architect / Landscape Designer; Environmental Illustrator; Art Educator (Environmental Focus); Public Art Project Coordinator (Green Projects); Green Set Designer; Eco-Fashion Designer; Sustainable Graphic Designer; Community Arts Organizer (Eco-Initiatives).

questions: What is the STEAM for Sustainability project?
What is Erasmus+ project 2023-1-HR01-KA220-SCH-000161027?
entries.answerShort: STEAM for Sustainability is an Erasmus+ project focused on raising awareness about environmental sustainability through STEAM education and green careers.
entries.answerLong: STEAM for Sustainability is an Erasmus+ project (no 2023-1-HR01-KA220-SCH-000161027) that aims to increase awareness among new generations about environmental sustainability. The project promotes green technologies and green jobs through the implementation of the STEAM field, innovative educational content, and the creation of chatbots in English.

questions: What is the main goal of the STEAM for Sustainability project?
What does the project aim to achieve?
entries.answerShort: The project aims to raise awareness about sustainability and increase interest in green technologies and green jobs, especially among girls.
entries.answerLong: The project aims to increase awareness of environmental sustainability among students and teachers. It promotes interest in green technologies and green jobs within the STEAM field by creating chatbots, innovative teaching materials, and through dissemination activities. A special focus is placed on increasing the interest of girls in green occupations.

questions: What activities are included in the project?
How is the project implemented?
entries.answerShort: The project includes virtual meetings, mobility sessions, workshops, lectures, expert presentations, visits, and professional excursions.
entries.answerLong: During 36 months, the project includes 7 virtual meetings and 5 mobility sessions. Activities include 22 workshops, 1 lecture, 2 expert presentations, 6 visits to relevant institutions, and 2 professional excursions. At least 200 students and 50 teachers participate in the project.

questions: Who is the project intended for?
Who participates in the project?
entries.answerShort: The project is intended for students and teachers of Josip Slavenski Čakovec Gymnasium and partner schools.
entries.answerLong: The project is intended for students and teachers of Josip Slavenski Čakovec Gymnasium and partner schools from Greece, Italy, Lithuania, and Poland. At least 200 students and 50 teachers participate in project activities.

questions: Which countries participate in the project?
Who are the project partners?
entries.answerShort: The project partners are schools from Croatia, Italy, Greece, Lithuania, and Poland.
entries.answerLong: The project is coordinated by Josip Slavenski Čakovec Gymnasium (Croatia) and includes partner schools from Italy (ITET Leonardo da Vinci – Milazzo), Greece (3 Geniko Lykeio Galatsi), Lithuania (Vilniaus Žvėryno gimnazija), and Poland (Janusz Korczak High School in Wieluń).

questions: Who coordinates the project in Croatia?
Who is the project coordinator?
entries.answerShort: The project coordinator is Vedrana Močnik from Josip Slavenski Čakovec Gymnasium.
entries.answerLong: The project coordinator for Croatia is Vedrana Močnik, a Croatian language teacher at Josip Slavenski Čakovec Gymnasium. The school coordinates the project together with partner schools from Greece, Italy, Lithuania, and Poland.

questions: Which mobilities are included in the project?
Where have mobility sessions taken place?
entries.answerShort: Mobility sessions include visits to Greece, Lithuania, Poland, and Croatia.
entries.answerLong: The project includes mobility sessions to partner countries. Mobilities have taken place in Greece, Lithuania, Poland, and Croatia as part of international cooperation and learning activities.

questions: How many virtual meetings are planned?
What are virtual meetings in the project?
entries.answerShort: The project includes 7 virtual meetings where partners collaborate online.
entries.answerLong: The project includes 7 virtual meetings during its 36-month duration. These online meetings allow partner schools to collaborate, plan activities, share results, and coordinate implementation.

questions: How are project results promoted?
How does the project promote sustainability?
entries.answerShort: Results are disseminated through promotion, social media, workshops, and educational materials.
entries.answerLong: The project results are disseminated and promoted throughout implementation. This includes educational workshops, presentations, mobility activities, and promotion through online platforms such as Instagram and school websites.

questions: How long does the STEAM for Sustainability project last?
What is the duration of the project?
entries.answerShort: The project lasts 36 months.
entries.answerLong: The STEAM for Sustainability Erasmus+ project runs for 36 months, during which multiple educational, virtual, and mobility activities are implemented.

questions: What does STEAM stand for?
entries.answerShort: STEAM stands for Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics.
entries.answerLong: STEAM stands for Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics. It promotes interdisciplinary learning and problem-solving through creative and scientific approaches.

questions: Why is STEAM important for sustainability?
entries.answerShort: STEAM connects science, technology and creativity to solve environmental problems.
entries.answerLong: STEAM is important for sustainability because it integrates scientific knowledge, technological innovation, engineering solutions, creative thinking and mathematics to address environmental challenges and promote green solutions.

questions: How does the project benefit students?
entries.answerShort: It develops green skills, digital skills and international cooperation experience.
entries.answerLong: Students improve their environmental awareness, digital literacy, teamwork, communication skills and knowledge about green jobs while collaborating with peers from other European countries.

questions: How does the project benefit teachers?
entries.answerShort: Teachers gain innovative teaching materials and international collaboration experience.
entries.answerLong: Teachers participate in workshops, expert lectures and mobility sessions, gaining new methodologies, digital tools and teaching strategies related to sustainability and STEAM education.

questions: Why was a chatbot created in this project?
entries.answerShort: To make learning about sustainability interactive and accessible.
entries.answerLong: The chatbot was created to provide students and visitors with quick, interactive access to information about sustainability, green jobs and project activities in English.

questions: What digital tools are used in the project?
entries.answerShort: Chatbots, online meetings, social media and digital educational materials.
entries.answerLong: The project uses digital tools such as chatbots, virtual meetings platforms, online collaboration tools, social media channels and interactive educational content.

questions: What is the purpose of mobility sessions?
entries.answerShort: To exchange knowledge, culture and best practices among partner schools.
entries.answerLong: Mobility sessions allow students and teachers to visit partner schools, participate in workshops, explore green technologies and experience different educational systems and cultures.

questions: Why is international cooperation important in this project?
entries.answerShort: Because sustainability is a global challenge that requires shared solutions.
entries.answerLong: International cooperation enables students and teachers to share perspectives, compare environmental challenges across countries and collaboratively develop innovative sustainable solutions.

questions: Why does the project focus on girls in green jobs?
entries.answerShort: To reduce gender gaps in STEAM and green careers.
entries.answerLong: The project encourages girls to pursue green and STEAM-related careers by increasing awareness, providing role models and promoting equal opportunities in sustainable industries.

questions: How does the project promote green careers?
entries.answerShort: Through workshops, expert lectures and real-world examples.
entries.answerLong: The project introduces students to green professions through expert presentations, visits to relevant institutions, mobility sessions and educational materials focused on sustainable technologies.

questions: How many workshops are planned in the project?
entries.answerShort: 22 workshops are planned.
entries.answerLong: The project includes 22 workshops that focus on sustainability, green technologies, digital innovation and STEAM-based learning.

questions: When did the project start?
entries.answerShort: The project started in 2023.
entries.answerLong: The Erasmus+ project STEAM for Sustainability began in 2023 and runs for 36 months.

questions: What long-term impact does the project aim to have?
entries.answerShort: To inspire sustainable thinking and future green professionals.
entries.answerLong: The project aims to build long-term environmental awareness, encourage sustainable lifestyles and inspire students to pursue green and STEAM-related careers.

questions: Where can we follow the project online?
entries.answerShort: On Instagram and partner school websites.
entries.answerLong: The project can be followed on Instagram (steamproject2023) and through official partner school websites and social media platforms.

questions: How does the project involve the local community?
entries.answerShort: Through dissemination, public presentations and school activities.
entries.answerLong: The project promotes sustainability through community events, presentations, workshops and online promotion to engage the wider public.

questions: How does the project raise environmental awareness?
entries.answerShort: By teaching sustainability through interactive and practical activities.
entries.answerLong: Environmental awareness is raised through workshops, expert lectures, mobility activities, digital content and the chatbot platform.

questions: What makes this project innovative?
entries.answerShort: It combines STEAM education, sustainability and chatbot technology.
entries.answerLong: The project is innovative because it integrates interdisciplinary STEAM education with sustainability topics and uses chatbot technology for interactive learning.

questions: What green skills do students develop in the project?
entries.answerShort: Energy awareness, recycling knowledge, digital sustainability skills.
entries.answerLong: Students develop skills such as energy efficiency awareness, sustainable resource management, recycling knowledge, problem-solving and digital competencies related to environmental sustainability.

questions: What is Erasmus+?
entries.answerShort: Erasmus+ is a European Union program supporting education and international cooperation.
entries.answerLong: Erasmus+ is a European Union funding program that supports education, training, youth and sport initiatives across Europe, promoting mobility and cooperation between institutions.
